brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is variational quantum optimization for topological data analysis — encoding combinatorial-Laplacian null-space (Betti-number) counting as a PCE Rayleigh-quotient minimization with deflation over a qubit register. Paper B's machinery is kinetic Boltzmann transport theory — a Liouville operator plus collision integrals with thermal masses governing bubble-wall friction in a plasma. These are unrelated formalisms (linear-algebraic homology estimation vs. out-of-equilibrium transport), with no role-for-role correspondence between their variables, driving equations, or objects; the only overlap is the loose word 'transition' used in entirely different senses.
